refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency percentile and end-to-end scope, focusing on measurable performance for safety-critical teleoperation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication path under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during representative operational scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Optimize data processing and transmission within the teleoperation system to reduce internal delays.
FR3: Ensure timely delivery and processing of telemetry and tasking messages to maintain latency requirements.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use a dedicated low-latency network link with QoS guarantees.
DP2: Implement real-time data compression and prioritization in teleoperation data streams.
DP3: Design teleoperation software modules with asynchronous, event-driven processing to minimize internal buffering delays.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combine DP1, DP2, and DP3 to holistically address latency from network to software processing.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between network link and teleoperation software processing.
Dependency on telemetry and tasking message formats.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interfaces to minimize cross-domain interference.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed parameters for latency guarantees.
Risk of software processing delays if asynchronous design is not properly implemented.
Risk of increased complexity in data compression affecting real-time constraints.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor implementation complexity and validate latency performance iteratively to adjust DP selections if needed.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realizes teleoperation data processing, compression, prioritization, and internal communication to meet latency requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets with latency and QoS constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives vehicle state data necessary for teleoperation feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends operator commands to the vehicle.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of teleoperation logic and latency-sensitive processing; low diffusion risk.
Vehicle module handles physical actuation and telemetry but less suitable for latency optimization logic.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not directly control teleoperation latency.
Overall system module but too broad for focused latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency-critical teleoperation logic is best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and enable focused optimization.
containment_risks: Potential interface latency if Network Link interface is not tightly integrated.
Risk of cross-module dependencies if telemetry or tasking interfaces are not well defined.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure strict interface contracts and performance monitoring at Network Link and Teleoperation boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched by the selected DPs addressing network and software latency aspects with clear verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as latency-critical logic is centralized in Teleoperation module.
Potential risk if Network Link interface performance degrades.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for latency control.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end using telemetry and network monitoring tools at the Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-related functions to Teleoperation IM with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface performance requirements for Network Link.
Plan iterative latency testing during implementation.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently captures the latency requirement with matched DPs and IM assignment. Verification and containment risks are manageable with planned revisions.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Optimize data processing and transmission within the teleoperation system to reduce internal delays.
FR3: Ensure timely delivery and processing of telemetry and tasking messages to maintain latency requirements.

revise.why: Refine interface contracts and performance monitoring at Network Link and Teleoperation boundaries to mitigate containment risks.

assumptions: Network conditions meet agreed latency parameters.
Teleoperation software can be designed with asynchronous, event-driven processing.
open_risks: Potential latency spikes due to network variability.
Implementation complexity of real-time compression and prioritization.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under representative network conditions.
Performance benchmarks of teleoperation software modules.
